title: Mock Exam D
description: A 22-task RHCSA9 mock exam covering persistent networking, repositories, users, services, storage, NFS, SSH, and rootless containers across client and server.

tasks: On client, recover root access from the console and set the root password to cinder9.

On client, configure persistent IPv4 networking.

IP address: 192.168.122.43/24
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: client-d.exam9.lab
On client, configure enabled BaseOS and AppStream repositories from http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
On client, install tree from the configured repositories and remove tcpdump if it is installed.
On client, create group opsd9. Create users anad9, devd9, and auditd9; auditd9 must use /sbin/nologin. Set each password to cinder9 and add anad9 and devd9 to opsd9.
On client, set maximum password age 60 days and warning period 7 days for anad9. Allow members of opsd9 to run /usr/bin/systemctl with sudo without a password.
On client, create /srv/opsd9 owned by root:opsd9 with permissions 2770 and a default ACL granting opsd9 full access.
On client, create executable script /usr/local/bin/report-d9 that writes drift ember report and the active state of sshd, chronyd, and firewalld to /root/report-d9.txt.
On client, create a 512 MiB swap file at /swapd9, activate it immediately, and make it persistent.
On client, create volume group vgd9 on /dev/sdb, create logical volume datad9 with size 320 MiB, format it as XFS, and mount it persistently at /mnt/datad9.
On client, create user podd9, enable lingering for that user, and run a rootless container named webd9 from localhost/rhcsa-httpd-base:latest.

On server, configure persistent IPv4 networking.

IP address: 192.168.122.3/24
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: server-d.exam9.lab
On server, configure enabled BaseOS and AppStream repositories from http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
On server, create group srvd9, create user svcd9 with password cinder9, add svcd9 to srvd9, and allow srvd9 to run /usr/bin/systemctl with sudo without a password.
On server, publish /var/www/html/exam-d.html containing drift portal web, configure httpd to listen on TCP port 8303, label the port for httpd, and open it permanently in firewalld.
On server, enable persistent systemd journal storage and restart systemd-journald.
On server, schedule a root cron job that runs every 8 minutes and appends drift watch cron to /var/log/auditd9.log.
On server, set the default boot target to multi-user.target and create /srv/server-d9 owned by root:srvd9 with permissions 2770.
On server, export /exports/rhcsa9-d to 192.168.122.0/24 with a README containing drift team export. On client, mount server:/exports/rhcsa9-d persistently at /mnt/rhcsa9-d.
On server, create user copyd9 with password cinder9. On client, configure key-based SSH login for root to copyd9@server.
On client, create /root/exam-d-copy.txt containing drift packet transfer and copy it to server:/home/copyd9/exam-d-copy.txt.
On server, enable chronyd for the lab network. On client, configure chronyd to use server as its only time source.
solution_commands: # At the boot menu, edit the selected kernel entry.
# Append rw init=/bin/bash to the linux line and boot with Ctrl+x.
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.43/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
for service in sshd chronyd firewalld; do
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.3/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
checks: echo cinder9 | su - root -c 'whoami' 2>/dev/null | grep -qx root

hostnamectl --static | grep -qx client-d.exam9.lab && CONN="System eth1"; nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.43/24 && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.1 && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fq 192.168.122.3

test -f /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '^baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/?$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '^baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/?$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && [ "$(grep -Ec '^enabled=1$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ] && [ "$(grep -Ec '^gpgcheck=0$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ]
rpm -q tree >/dev/null

getent group opsd9 >/dev/null && id -nG anad9 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx opsd9 && id -nG devd9 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx opsd9 && getent passwd auditd9 | awk -F: '$7 == "/sbin/nologin" {found=1} END {exit !found}'

chage -l anad9 | grep -Eq 'Maximum.*60' && chage -l anad9 | grep -Eq 'warning.*7' && grep -Eq '^%opsd9[[:space:]]+ALL=\(ALL\)[[:space:]]+NOPASSWD:[[:space:]]*/usr/bin/systemctl$' /etc/sudoers.d/opsd9-systemctl
stat -c '%U:%G:%a' /srv/opsd9 | grep -qx root:opsd9:2770 && getfacl -p /srv/opsd9 | grep -Eq '^default:group:opsd9:rwx$'
test -x /usr/local/bin/report-d9 && /usr/local/bin/report-d9 >/dev/null && grep -Fxq 'drift ember report' /root/report-d9.txt
swapon --show=NAME --noheadings | grep -qx '/swapd9' && awk '$1 == "/swapd9" && $2 == "swap" && $3 == "swap" {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
lvs /dev/vgd9/datad9 >/dev/null 2>&1 && findmnt -no TARGET /mnt/datad9 | grep -qx /mnt/datad9 && awk '$2 == "/mnt/datad9" && $3 == "xfs" {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
runuser -l podd9 -c 'podman ps --format {{.Names}}' | grep -qx webd9 && loginctl show-user podd9 | grep -Eq '^Linger=yes$'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; hostnamectl --static | grep -qx server-d.exam9.lab && CONN="System eth1"; nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.3/24 && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.1 && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fq 192.168.122.3'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; test -f /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '"'"'^baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/?$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '"'"'^baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/?$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && [ "$(grep -Ec '"'"'^enabled=1$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ] && [ "$(grep -Ec '"'"'^gpgcheck=0$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ]'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getent group srvd9 >/dev/null && id -nG svcd9 | tr '"'"' '"'"' '"'"'\n'"'"' | grep -qx srvd9 && grep -Eq '"'"'^%srvd9[[:space:]]+ALL=\(ALL\)[[:space:]]+NOPASSWD:[[:space:]]*/usr/bin/systemctl$'"'"' /etc/sudoers.d/srvd9-systemctl'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq '"'"'drift portal web'"'"' /var/www/html/exam-d.html && grep -Eq '"'"'^Listen[[:space:]]+8303$'"'"' /etc/httpd/conf.d/exam-d.conf && semanage port -l | awk '"'"'$1 == "http_port_t" && $2 == "tcp" && $0 ~ /(^|[ ,])8303([, ]|$)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' && firewall-cmd --permanent --query-port=8303/tcp && systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; files=""; test -d /var/log/journal && systemctl is-active systemd-journald | grep -qx active && files=$(find /etc/systemd -maxdepth 2 \( -path /etc/systemd/journald.conf -o -path '"'"'/etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*.conf'"'"' \) -type f 2>/dev/null); test -n "$files" && awk '"'"'FNR == 1 {in_journal=0} /^[[:space:]]*\[Journal\][[:space:]]*($|#)/ {in_journal=1; next} /^[[:space:]]*\[/ {in_journal=0} in_journal && /^[[:space:]]*Storage[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*persistent[[:space:]]*($|#)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' $files'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; test -x /usr/local/sbin/auditd9.sh && grep -Fxq '"'"'*/8 * * * * root /usr/local/sbin/auditd9.sh'"'"' /etc/cron.d/auditd9 && grep -Fxq "echo '"'"'drift watch cron'"'"' >> /var/log/auditd9.log" /usr/local/sbin/auditd9.sh && systemctl is-enabled crond | grep -qx enabled'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl get-default | grep -qx multi-user.target && stat -c '"'"'%U:%G:%a'"'"' /srv/server-d9 | grep -qx root:srvd9:2770'

findmnt -no SOURCE,TARGET /mnt/rhcsa9-d | grep -qx 'server:/exports/rhcsa9-d /mnt/rhcsa9-d' && grep -Eq '^server:/exports/rhcsa9-d[[:space:]]+/mnt/rhcsa9-d[[:space:]]+nfs([[:space:]]|$)' /etc/fstab && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq "drift team export" /exports/rhcsa9-d/README && grep -Eq "^/exports/rhcsa9-d[[:space:]]+192\\.168\\.122\\.0/24" /etc/exports.d/rhcsa9-d.exports && systemctl is-active nfs-server | grep -qx active'
runuser -l root -c 'ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o NumberOfPasswordPrompts=0 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null copyd9@server true'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq '"'"'drift packet transfer'"'"' /home/copyd9/exam-d-copy.txt'

grep -Eq '^server[[:space:]]+server[[:space:]]+iburst$' /etc/chrony.conf && systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active chronyd | grep -qx active'
